---
title: "Data Viewer Widget Roadmap"
format: html
---

# DataViewer Widget Development Roadmap

This document tracks remaining development tasks for the Data Viewer Widget. For documentation
of completed features and current architecture, see the [main developer documentation](index.qmd).

::: {.callout-note}
## Pre-Architecture Widget

The Data Viewer Widget was the first plot widget developed, before the unified `Plots/` directory
structure and common axis/interaction infrastructure. Several refactoring items below extract
DataViewer-specific code into shared components or adopt existing shared components from
`Plots/Common/`. These refactoring tasks are ordered by priority and can be interleaved with
feature work.
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Related GitHub Issues

The following issues track the primary feature work in this roadmap:

- [#223](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/223) — Extract reusable temporal X-axis (CenteredTimeAxis widget)
- [#227](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/227) — Mouse-wheel modifiers for Y scaling (global, per-lane, viewport zoom)
- [#247](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/247) — Y-axis scalebars and lane labels
- [#248](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/248) — Viewport culling and configurable lane sizing
:::

## Completed Work Summary

The following features are complete and documented in [index.qmd](index.qmd):

- **EditorState pattern**: `DataViewerState` with `DataViewerStateData` serialization via reflect-cpp
- **SeriesOptionsRegistry**: Generic type-safe per-series options for analog, event, and interval types
- **Rendering pipeline**: `SceneBuildingHelpers` → `RenderableBatch` → `PlottingOpenGL::SceneRenderer`
- **Layout system**: `CorePlotting::LayoutEngine` with `StackedLayoutStrategy`; spike sorter configuration for custom channel ordering; dual lane sizing policy (`FitToViewport` / `FixedHeight`) with configurable lane height and gap
- **Y-axis transform pipeline**: `TransformComposers` composing data normalization, user adjustments, layout positioning, global scaling
- **Interaction system**: `DataViewerInteractionManager` state machine (Normal, CreateInterval, ModifyInterval, CreateLine modes)
- **Interval editing**: Click-to-select, edge dragging, collision prevention, multi-timeframe support
- **Entity selection**: Multi-select via Ctrl+click in `DataViewerSelectionManager`
- **Coordinate transforms**: Unified `DataViewerCoordinates` class for canvas ↔ world ↔ time ↔ data conversions
- **Analog vertex cache**: Ring buffer for 26–130x faster scrolling rendering
- **SVG export**: Publication-quality export using the same scene building as OpenGL rendering
- **Time frame synchronization**: Cross-sampling-rate display (e.g., 30 kHz electrophysiology + 500 Hz video)
- **Grouped data**: Group loading with auto-calculated scaling and event spacing
- **Theme and grid**: Dark/Light themes, configurable grid overlay
- **DataManager observer**: Combo box population, stale key cleanup
- **Developer Debug Panel**: Real-time diagnostics for lane layout, scales, coordinate transforms, and viewport state

**Not yet implemented**: Common axis integration, Y pan/zoom for channel navigation, enhanced
per-type visual options, viewport culling, and shared component extraction described below.

---

## Phase 1: Layout Foundation {#phase-1-layout-foundation .completed}

**Status**: Complete.
**Issues**: [#248](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/248),
[#227](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/227)

**Goal**: Establish the lane sizing model that all subsequent Y-axis features depend on.

### 1.1 Y-Scale State Audit {.completed}

Merged `global_zoom` and `global_vertical_scale` into a single `global_y_scale` field.
Exposed as "Global Y Scale" in the UI and used consistently in all `TransformComposers` functions.

### 1.2 Configurable Lane Sizing with Dual Policy {.completed}

`StackedLayoutStrategy` supports `FitToViewport` (equal division, default) and `FixedHeight`
(fixed `lane_height` + `lane_gap` per lane; total extent may exceed viewport). Layout config
persisted in `DataViewerLayoutConfig` via reflect-cpp. State API: `setLaneSizingPolicy()`,
`setLaneHeight()`, `setLaneGap()`, `layoutConfigChanged()` signal.

**Key files**: `StackedLayoutStrategy.hpp/.cpp`, `DataViewerStateData.hpp`, `DataViewerState.hpp/.cpp`

### 1.3 Lane Sizing UI Controls {.completed}

"Layout Settings" group in `DataViewerPropertiesWidget`: policy combo box, lane height spinbox
(FixedHeight only), lane gap spinbox. Bidirectional sync via `layoutConfigChanged()`.

### 1.4 Verification {.completed}

32+ channels in `FitToViewport` all visible. `FixedHeight` shows subset with panning.
Workspace save/restore roundtrips layout config.

---

## Phase 1B: Rendering Pipeline Modernization {#phase-1b-rendering-pipeline .completed}

**Status**: Complete.
**Depends on**: [Phase 1](#phase-1-layout-foundation).

**Goal**: Migrate from `TimeSeriesViewState` to shared `ViewStateData` zoom/pan model.
Eliminate the performance bottleneck where Y-axis panning triggered full scene rebuild + GPU
re-upload every frame.

### Summary of Changes

**ViewStateData adoption** (1B.1): `DataViewerStateData::view` is now `CorePlotting::ViewStateData`
(`x_min`/`x_max` as `double`, `y_min`/`y_max`, `y_pan`, `y_zoom`). `global_y_scale` remains a
separate field in `DataViewerStateData` (data amplitude gain, not a view transform). DataViewer
defaults: `x_min=0, x_max=1000, y_min=-1, y_max=1`. Convenience methods on `DataViewerState`:
`timeStart()`, `timeEnd()`, `timeWidth()`, `timeCenter()`.

**Scene dirty guard** (1B.3, 1B.6): `OpenGLWidget` uses separate signal handlers:

- `timeWindowChanged` → `scene_dirty = true` (scene rebuild needed)
- `viewStateChanged` → matrix update only (Y pan/zoom is instant)
- `layoutConfigChanged` → `scene_dirty = true` (layout transforms baked into vertices)
- `global_y_scale` changes detected via cached comparison → `scene_dirty = true`

Scene rebuilds (`rebuildScene()`) only occur when `_cache_state.scene_dirty` is set.
Y-axis panning updates only the projection/view matrices.

**Caller migration** (1B.7): All files across `DataViewer_Widget/` updated to use `ViewStateData`
field names (`x_min`/`x_max`/`y_pan` with appropriate casts). `DataViewerCoordinates`,
`DataViewerInputHandler`, `DataViewerInteractionManager`, `DataViewerDebugPanel`,
`DataViewerPropertiesWidget`, `SVGExporter`, and all test files migrated.

**Key architectural decisions**:

- `x_zoom` fixed at 1.0, `x_pan` at 0.0 (DataViewer X-axis controlled externally via time bar)
- Lane sizing policy changes reset `y_pan` to 0 to prevent stranded viewport on mode switch
- `double` precision for time bounds sufficient for indices up to ~9×10¹⁵

---

## Phase 2: Y-Axis Navigation {#phase-2-y-axis-navigation}

**Status**: In progress (Phase 2.1 completed).
**Issues**: [#227](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/227) (wheel modifiers, per-lane scaling),
[#248](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/248) (viewport culling),
[#247](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/247) (axis synchronization)
**Depends on**: [Phase 1](#phase-1-layout-foundation) (`FixedHeight` lanes must exist for
Y pan/zoom to be meaningful), [Phase 1B](#phase-1b-rendering-pipeline) (pan/zoom must be
performant before adding more interaction modes).

**Goal**: Allow users to pan and zoom the Y-axis so they can view a subset of channels at
readable resolution, inspired by [EventPlotWidget](../EventPlotWidget/index.qmd)'s trial Y
navigation.

### 2.1 MultiLaneVerticalAxisWidget (Minimal Version) {.completed}

Create a new `MultiLaneVerticalAxisWidget` in `Plots/Common/` and integrate it into
DataViewer. This is a **standalone widget** — not a subclass of the existing single-range
`VerticalAxisWidget`, which continues to serve ScatterPlotWidget and EventPlotWidget
unchanged.

The existing `VerticalAxisWidget` manages a single `y_min`/`y_max` range with uniform tick
marks. DataViewer lanes have **independent amplitude scales** (each series has its own
`user_scale_factor`, data range, and normalization), so a single-range axis cannot
meaningfully represent per-lane amplitudes. Building the multi-lane widget from the start
avoids a throwaway integration cycle.

**New files**:

```
Plots/Common/MultiLaneVerticalAxisWidget/
├── Core/
│   ├── MultiLaneVerticalAxisStateData.hpp   # LaneAxisDescriptor + vector, serializable
│   └── MultiLaneVerticalAxisState.hpp       # QObject with lane update signals
├── MultiLaneVerticalAxisWidget.hpp/.cpp     # QPainter rendering: labels + separators
└── CMakeLists.txt
```

The widget follows the standard four-layer pattern (`StateData → State → Widget →
optional WithControls`) and is driven by a `std::vector<LaneAxisDescriptor>`:

```cpp
struct LaneAxisDescriptor {
    std::string lane_id;        ///< Series key in DataManager
    std::string label;          ///< Display name (channel name or series key)
    float y_center;             ///< Center Y position in world coordinates
    float y_half_height;        ///< Half-height of the lane in world coordinates
};
```

**Minimal rendering scope** (Phase 2 only):

- Channel name labels painted at each lane's center Y position
- Lane boundary separators (horizontal lines between lanes)
- Viewport-aware: only renders labels for lanes visible in the current Y viewport
- Fixed 50px width (matching existing `VerticalAxisWidget` `sizeHint`)

Scalebars, visibility toggles, and per-lane range annotations are deferred to
[Phase 3](#phase-3-scalebars-labels), which adds them incrementally to this same widget.

**Layout**: Place `MultiLaneVerticalAxisWidget` in a `QHBoxLayout` to the left of
`OpenGLWidget`:

```
DataViewer_Widget (QWidget)
├── QVBoxLayout
│   ├── QHBoxLayout [stretch 1]
│   │   ├── MultiLaneVerticalAxisWidget (50px fixed width)
│   │   └── OpenGLWidget [stretch 1]
│   └── (future: CenteredTimeAxisWidget — Phase 4)
```

**State integration**:

```cpp
// In DataViewerState
_multi_lane_axis_state = std::make_unique<MultiLaneVerticalAxisState>(this);
```

**Data flow** (one-directional — labels are read-only display):

- After each layout computation, DataViewer extracts `LaneAxisDescriptor` entries from
  `LayoutResponse` and pushes them to `MultiLaneVerticalAxisState`
- `DataViewerState::viewStateChanged` → widget repaint (for Y pan/zoom tracking)
- No bidirectional range sync is needed (Y pan/zoom originates from wheel events on
  `OpenGLWidget`, not from the axis widget)

**Files**: `src/WhiskerToolbox/Plots/Common/MultiLaneVerticalAxisWidget/` (new),
`src/WhiskerToolbox/DataViewer_Widget/Core/DataViewerState.hpp`,
`src/WhiskerToolbox/DataViewer_Widget/Core/DataViewerState.cpp`,
`src/WhiskerToolbox/DataViewer_Widget/Core/DataViewerStateData.hpp`,
`src/WhiskerToolbox/DataViewer_Widget/UI/DataViewer_Widget.cpp`

### 2.3 Viewport Visibility Culling {.completed}

When many channels are loaded but only a few are visible (e.g., viewing 4 of 32 channels),
non-visible series are culled from the rendering pipeline using **additive culling**:

1. **Layout query**: `LayoutResponse::isSeriesVisible()` and `visibleSeriesIds()` determine
   which lanes overlap the effective Y viewport (accounting for `y_pan`).
2. **Additive tracking**: `SceneCacheState::series_in_scene` tracks which series have been
   built into the current scene. The set accumulates across pan-triggered rebuilds and is
   only cleared on full data changes (time window, layout config, global Y scale).
3. **Pan-triggered rebuild**: `hasNewVisibleSeries()` checks if any enabled, viewport-visible
   series is not yet in `series_in_scene`. If so, an additive rebuild is triggered that
   builds the new series plus all previously-built series.
4. **Additive batch building**: `add*BatchesToBuilder` methods skip a series only if it is
   both off-screen AND not in `series_in_scene`. This ensures panning never removes
   already-rendered series from the scene.

Non-stackable series (full-canvas intervals, full-canvas events) always pass the visibility
check because their layout spans the entire viewport.

**Key files**: `src/CorePlotting/Layout/LayoutEngine.hpp` (`isSeriesVisible`, `visibleSeriesIds`),
`src/CorePlotting/Layout/LayoutEngine.cpp`,
`src/WhiskerToolbox/DataViewer_Widget/Rendering/OpenGLWidget.hpp` (`SceneCacheState::series_in_scene`, `hasNewVisibleSeries`),
`src/WhiskerToolbox/DataViewer_Widget/Rendering/OpenGLWidget.cpp`

### 2.4 Wheel Modifier Chords for Y Interaction

::: {.callout-important}
## Preserving Existing Behavior

The current default mouse wheel behavior (time-axis zoom) must be preserved. Users who do
not use modifiers should see no change. Y-axis panning is already handled by left-click
drag (via `DataViewerInputHandler`) and does not need a wheel chord.
:::

**Current behavior** (unchanged):

| Modifier | Action |
|----------|--------|
| None | Time-axis zoom (normal sensitivity) |
| Shift | Time-axis zoom (fine sensitivity) |
| Ctrl | Time-axis zoom (coarse sensitivity) |
| Left-drag | Y-axis pan |

**New chords** — all Y-axis wheel actions share `Alt` as the common prefix, distinguishing
them from X-axis zoom (no `Alt`):

| Modifier | Action | Scope | State Field |
|----------|--------|-------|-------------|
| Alt+Scroll | Global Y scale | All lanes | `global_y_scale` |
| Alt+Shift+Scroll | Per-lane Y scale | Hovered lane | `user_scale_factor` in `AnalogSeriesOptionsData` |
| Alt+Ctrl+Scroll | Y viewport zoom (cursor-anchored) | View matrix | `y_zoom` in `ViewStateData` |

**Global Y scale** (`Alt+Scroll`): Adjusts `global_y_scale`, which amplifies all series
uniformly within their lanes. This is a data gain factor baked into vertices, so it triggers
a scene rebuild.

**Per-lane Y scale** (`Alt+Shift+Scroll`): Adjusts the `user_scale_factor` for the analog
series under the cursor. Uses `OpenGLWidget::findSeriesAtPosition()` for hit testing.
No-op if the cursor is not over an analog lane. The per-series scale is already serialized
and shown in the properties panel, so this provides a mouse-driven shortcut for an existing
feature. `Shift` narrows the scope from global to local.

**Y viewport zoom** (`Alt+Ctrl+Scroll`): Adjusts `y_zoom` in `ViewStateData`, which scales
the view matrix — making lane heights appear larger or smaller without changing the data
gain. This is a cheap matrix-only update (no scene rebuild). Cursor-anchored: the focal
point under the mouse remains stable during zoom. Define min/max span clamps so projection
and hit testing never degenerate.

**Implementation notes**:

- `Alt+click` is window-move on some Linux WMs, but `Alt+Scroll` is generally safe
- Respect `OpenGLWidget::isInteractionActive()` (no zoom while creating/editing intervals)
- When multiple modifiers match (e.g., `Alt+Ctrl+Shift`), the most specific chord wins:
  per-lane (`Alt+Shift`) > viewport zoom (`Alt+Ctrl`) > global scale (`Alt`)
- Document chords in a user-facing summary (tooltip, status bar, or User Guide stub)

**Spinbox synchronization**: The properties panel already has spinboxes for `global_y_scale`
and per-series `user_scale_factor`. Wheel-driven changes must update these spinboxes without
triggering feedback loops. Follow the bidirectional sync pattern used by
`VerticalAxisState` / `HorizontalAxisState`:

- **Wheel → State → Spinbox**: Wheel handler calls a "silent" setter on `DataViewerState`
  (e.g., `setGlobalYScaleSilent()`), which updates the value and emits a `*Updated` signal
  (not `*Changed`). The spinbox connects to `*Updated` and sets its value with signals
  blocked (or via an anti-recursion guard).
- **Spinbox → State → Scene**: User edits spinbox → emits `valueChanged` → calls the normal
  setter (e.g., `setGlobalYScale()`), which emits `*Changed` and triggers a scene rebuild.
- The same dual-signal pattern (`Changed` for user input, `Updated` for programmatic sync)
  prevents wheel → spinbox → state → scene feedback loops.

**Files**: `src/WhiskerToolbox/DataViewer_Widget/UI/DataViewer_Widget.cpp` (`wheelEvent`),
`src/WhiskerToolbox/DataViewer_Widget/Core/DataViewerState.cpp`,
`src/WhiskerToolbox/DataViewer_Widget/Core/SeriesOptionsRegistry.cpp`,
`src/WhiskerToolbox/DataViewer_Widget/UI/DataViewerPropertiesWidget.cpp`

### 2.5 Visible Lane Indicator in Properties Panel

Add a read-only "Visible lanes: N of M" label to `DataViewerPropertiesWidget` that updates
whenever the viewport or lane count changes. This provides at-a-glance context for Y
navigation without requiring single-range spinbox controls (which don't map naturally to a
multi-lane axis).

**Files**: `src/WhiskerToolbox/DataViewer_Widget/UI/DataViewerPropertiesWidget.cpp`,
`src/WhiskerToolbox/DataViewer_Widget/UI/DataViewerPropertiesWidget.ui`

### 2.6 Verification

- Load 32+ channels in `FixedHeight` mode.
- Y pan scrolls through lanes; Y zoom narrows the visible range.
- Viewport culling skips off-screen series (verify via Developer Debug Panel rendering stats).
- Per-lane hover scaling adjusts the hovered series only.
- Channel name labels appear on the `MultiLaneVerticalAxisWidget` at correct Y positions.
- Labels scroll correctly during Y pan and recompute when series are added/removed.
- "Visible lanes: N of M" indicator updates correctly.
- Workspace save/restore roundtrips all new state.
- Existing `DataViewer_Widget` tests that assert time window changes after wheel remain green.
- Existing `VerticalAxisWidget` consumers (ScatterPlot, EventPlot) are unaffected.

---

## Phase 3: Y-Axis Scalebars and Enhanced Lane Annotations {#phase-3-scalebars-labels}

**Status**: Not started.
**Issues**: [#247](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/247)
**Depends on**: [Phase 2](#phase-2-y-axis-navigation) (`MultiLaneVerticalAxisWidget` exists
with minimal label + separator rendering)

**Goal**: Extend the `MultiLaneVerticalAxisWidget` created in Phase 2.1 with per-lane
scalebars, visibility toggles, and smart label positioning so users can interpret amplitudes
and identify individual traces at a glance.

::: {.callout-note}
## Incremental Enhancement — No Widget Replacement

Phase 2.1 creates the `MultiLaneVerticalAxisWidget` with channel name labels and lane
separators. All Phase 3 work is **additive** to that same widget — extending
`LaneAxisDescriptor` with amplitude range fields, adding scalebar rendering to
`paintEvent()`, and wiring new toggle controls. No widget is replaced or discarded.
:::

### 3.1 Extend LaneAxisDescriptor with Amplitude Information

Add per-lane amplitude fields to `LaneAxisDescriptor` so the widget can render scalebars:

```cpp
struct LaneAxisDescriptor {
    std::string lane_id;        ///< Series key in DataManager
    std::string label;          ///< Display name (channel name or series key)
    float y_center;             ///< Center Y position in world coordinates
    float y_half_height;        ///< Half-height of the lane in world coordinates
    double amplitude_min;       ///< Data-space minimum (after user_scale_factor)
    double amplitude_max;       ///< Data-space maximum (after user_scale_factor)
    std::string unit;           ///< Amplitude unit label (e.g., "µV", "mV")
    bool show_scalebar = true;  ///< Per-lane scalebar visibility
};
```

DataViewer populates the new fields from per-series statistics, `user_scale_factor`, and
group scaling normalization during layout computation.

**Files**: `src/WhiskerToolbox/Plots/Common/MultiLaneVerticalAxisWidget/Core/MultiLaneVerticalAxisStateData.hpp`

### 3.2 Per-Lane Y-Axis Scalebars

Within each lane's Y band in the axis widget, draw a small scalebar indicating the amplitude
range:

- A vertical bar with two endpoints and a magnitude label (e.g., "100 µV")
- The scale is computed from the lane's amplitude range: `user_scale_factor`, data statistics,
  and any group scaling normalization
- Scalebars provide absolute scale context that uniform tick marks cannot (since each lane
  has its own amplitude mapping)

### 3.3 Visibility Toggles

Add options to `DataViewerStateData` and the properties UI:

- Toggle lane labels on/off
- Toggle scalebars on/off
- These should be serialized for workspace restore

### 3.4 Group Scaling Verification

Verify that scalebars update correctly when switching between individual and unified group
scaling modes (`GroupScalingState`), and that labels remain aligned during Y pan/zoom.

### 3.5 Smart Label Positioning

Labels and scalebars must be viewport-aware
([#248](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/248)):

- When panning in Y, labels for visible lanes must adjust accordingly
- Labels for off-screen lanes should not be rendered
- Labels should not overlap when lanes are very short (FixedHeight with small lane_height
  or FitToViewport with many channels)

### 3.6 Verification

- Each data lane can render an optional Y-axis scalebar indicating its magnitude/units.
- Each data lane displays its assigned name/identifier (e.g., channel name or series key).
- Scalebars and labels toggle on/off via properties UI.
- Correct behavior when switching between individual and unified group scaling modes.
- Labels remain aligned and visible during vertical panning.

---

## Phase 4: CenteredTimeAxis Widget {#phase-4-centered-time-axis}

**Status**: Not started.
**Issues**: [#223](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/223)
**Parallel with**: [Phase 3](#phase-3-scalebars-labels) (no dependency between X and Y axis work)

**Goal**: Extract the DataViewer's temporal X-axis behavior into a reusable `CenteredTimeAxisWidget`
in `Plots/Common/`, following the established four-layer axis widget pattern
(`StateData → State → Widget → WithRangeControls`). The `SpectrogramWidget` (currently a stub
with time-axis state defined but no axis widget) is the primary second consumer.

### 4.1 CenteredTimeAxis — New Horizontal Axis Variant

The DataViewer's X-axis behavior is unique among the plot widgets: it is **always centered on the
global time position** with zoom but **no independent X panning**. Neither the existing
`RelativeTimeAxis` (event-relative, symmetric window) nor `HorizontalAxis` (arbitrary range with
pan) match this pattern exactly.

A new `CenteredTimeAxis` variant should be added to `Plots/Common/`:

```
Plots/Common/CenteredTimeAxisWidget/
├── Core/
│   ├── CenteredTimeAxisStateData.hpp   # half_window (zoom level), serializable
│   └── CenteredTimeAxisState.hpp       # QObject with zoom math + signals
├── CenteredTimeAxisWidget.hpp          # Axis display (tick marks, labels) via QPainter
└── CenteredTimeAxisWithRangeControls.hpp  # Factory with zoom spinbox
```

**State fields:**
```cpp
struct CenteredTimeAxisStateData {
    double time_start = 0.0;   ///< Visible window start in time units
    double time_end = 10000.0; ///< Visible window end in time units
};
```

The state uses `time_start`/`time_end` rather than `half_window` for consistency with the
existing `TimeSeriesViewState` representation. The State class provides convenience accessors
for `halfWindow()` and `center()` computed from these fields.

**Key behavior differences from existing axes:**

| Aspect | CenteredTimeAxis | RelativeTimeAxis | HorizontalAxis |
|--------|-----------------|------------------|----------------|
| Center | Global time position (external) | 0 (alignment event) | User-adjustable |
| Pan | None — follows time bar | User-adjustable | User-adjustable |
| Zoom | Adjusts window width symmetrically around center | Adjusts min/max symmetrically | Adjusts x_min/x_max |
| Labels | Absolute time values | Relative offsets (±N) | Arbitrary values |

**Signals:** `rangeChanged` (user action), `rangeUpdated` (silent sync — matches bidirectional
sync pattern used by all axis widgets)

The axis widget receives the current time position from an external source (the application's
global time bar emits `timeChanged`). On each time change, the parent widget recenters the
window: `time_start = current_time - half_window; time_end = current_time + half_window`.

**Sharing potential:** This axis variant could also be useful for any future widget that needs a
time-following view (e.g., a real-time signal monitor).

### 4.2 Zoom Math Extraction

The wheel-zoom math currently lives in `DataViewer_Widget::wheelEvent` and
`DataViewerState::adjustTimeWidth`. This logic should be extracted into
`CenteredTimeAxisState` as reusable methods:

- `applyWheelZoom(int angle_delta, ZoomScalingMode mode, Qt::KeyboardModifiers mods, int64_t total_frames)`
- Supports both `Adaptive` and `Fixed` scaling modes with `Shift` (fine) / `Ctrl` (coarse) modifiers
- Optional clamp policy hooks
- Pure logic that can be unit-tested without QWidget

This addresses the core goal of
[#223](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/223): making the zoom behavior
reusable without duplicating the logic in each consumer widget.

### 4.3 DataViewer Migration

Migrate `DataViewer_Widget` to use `CenteredTimeAxisWidget`:

1. Add `CenteredTimeAxisState` member to `DataViewerState`
2. Place `CenteredTimeAxisWidget` below the OpenGL canvas in the `QVBoxLayout`
3. Replace `DataViewerState::setTimeWindow()` / `adjustTimeWindowWidth()` with
   `CenteredTimeAxisState` methods
4. Wire state sync: `CenteredTimeAxisState` → `DataViewerState` time window updates
5. Update `DataViewerStateData` serialization to include `CenteredTimeAxisStateData`

Existing tests that assert time window changes after wheel events must remain green.

### 4.4 SpectrogramWidget Adoption

Adopt the same `CenteredTimeAxisWidget` for `SpectrogramWidget`'s time axis. The
SpectrogramWidget currently defines `SpectrogramViewState` with `x_min`/`x_max`/`x_zoom`/`x_pan`
but has no axis widget — it is essentially a stub GUI. This provides:

- Tick marks and time labels via QPainter (matching other plot widgets)
- Zoom controls via `CenteredTimeAxisWithRangeControls`
- Consistent wheel-zoom behavior shared with DataViewer

### 4.5 Tests and Documentation

- Port/add unit tests for zoom math (adaptive vs fixed scaling, modifiers) independent of QWidget
- Update `TimeRange.hpp` / `TimeSeriesViewState` comments to point to the new module
- Developer documentation entry for the shared temporal axis
- Link from DataViewer and Spectrogram developer docs

### 4.6 Verification

- DataViewer time-axis behavior unchanged (behavioral parity with pre-migration)
- `CenteredTimeAxisWidget` renders absolute time tick marks and labels below the OpenGL canvas
- SpectrogramWidget can use the same axis widget for its time dimension
- Wheel zoom tests pass for both consumers
- Workspace save/restore roundtrips `CenteredTimeAxisStateData`

---

## Phase 5: Enhanced Event Glyph Options {#phase-5-enhanced-event-glyph-options}

**Status**: Not started. Events are currently rendered as simple ticks with configurable color
and spacing but no glyph type selection.

**Goal**: Full control over event glyph appearance, using the shared `CorePlotting::GlyphStyleData`
infrastructure that is now implemented and integrated into TemporalProjectionViewWidget,
OnionSkinViewWidget, and EventPlotWidget.

### 5.1 Shared GlyphStyleData (Complete)

The shared glyph infrastructure is now available:

| Component | Location | Status |
|-----------|----------|--------|
| `CorePlotting::GlyphType` enum | `CorePlotting/DataTypes/GlyphStyleData.hpp` | **Complete** |
| `CorePlotting::GlyphStyleData` struct | `CorePlotting/DataTypes/GlyphStyleData.hpp` | **Complete** |
| `CorePlotting::toRenderableGlyphType()` | `CorePlotting/DataTypes/GlyphStyleConversion.hpp` | **Complete** |
| `CorePlotting::hexColorToVec4()` | `CorePlotting/DataTypes/GlyphStyleConversion.hpp` | **Complete** |
| `GlyphStyleState` | `Plots/Common/GlyphStyleWidget/Core/GlyphStyleState.hpp` | **Complete** |
| `GlyphStyleControls` | `Plots/Common/GlyphStyleWidget/GlyphStyleControls.hpp` | **Complete** |

Already integrated into: TemporalProjectionViewWidget, OnionSkinViewWidget, EventPlotWidget.

### 5.2 State and Options Update

Extend `DigitalEventSeriesOptionsData` to include glyph options using the shared struct:

```cpp
#include "CorePlotting/DataTypes/GlyphStyleData.hpp"

struct DigitalEventSeriesOptionsData {
    rfl::Flatten<CorePlotting::SeriesStyle> style;
    
    // Event-specific settings (existing)
    EventPlottingModeData plotting_mode;
    float vertical_spacing;
    float event_height;
    float margin_factor;

    // Glyph options (using shared infrastructure)
    CorePlotting::GlyphStyleData glyph_style{
        CorePlotting::GlyphType::Tick, 5.0f, "#FFFFFF", 1.0f};
};
```

### 5.3 Properties Panel Controls

Embed `GlyphStyleControls` from `Plots/Common/GlyphStyleWidget/` for per-event-key glyph
customization, following the same pattern used by TemporalProjectionViewWidget and
OnionSkinViewWidget:

```cpp
#include "Plots/Common/GlyphStyleWidget/GlyphStyleControls.hpp"

auto * controls = new GlyphStyleControls(glyph_style_state, parent);
```

### 5.4 Scene Building Update

Update `SceneBuildingHelpers::buildEventSeriesBatch()` to use
`CorePlotting::toRenderableGlyphType()` and `CorePlotting::hexColorToVec4()` from
`GlyphStyleConversion.hpp`, replacing the hardcoded tick rendering.

---

## Phase 6: Enhanced Interval Rendering {#phase-6-enhanced-interval-rendering}

**Status**: Not started. Intervals are currently rendered as filled rectangles with configurable
color and alpha but no border or pattern options.

**Goal**: Rich interval visualization with adjustable border, alpha, and hatching patterns.

### 6.1 IntervalRenderingOptions

Extend `DigitalIntervalSeriesOptionsData`:

```cpp
struct DigitalIntervalSeriesOptionsData {
    rfl::Flatten<CorePlotting::SeriesStyle> style;
    
    // Existing
    bool extend_full_canvas = true;
    float margin_factor = 0.95f;
    float interval_height = 1.0f;

    // New border options
    float border_thickness = 1.0f;        ///< Border line width in pixels (0 = no border)
    std::string border_color = "#FFFFFF"; ///< Border color in hex
    float border_alpha = 1.0f;            ///< Border alpha (separate from fill alpha)

    // New fill options
    float fill_alpha = 0.5f;             ///< Fill alpha (replaces general alpha for fill)

    // New hatching options
    IntervalHatchPattern hatch_pattern = IntervalHatchPattern::None;
    float hatch_spacing = 8.0f;          ///< Spacing between hatch lines in pixels
    float hatch_angle = 45.0f;           ///< Angle of hatch lines in degrees
    std::string hatch_color = "#FFFFFF"; ///< Hatch line color
};
```

### 6.2 Hatching Patterns

```cpp
enum class IntervalHatchPattern {
    None,              ///< Solid fill only (default)
    ForwardDiagonal,   ///< Lines from bottom-left to top-right
    BackwardDiagonal,  ///< Lines from top-left to bottom-right
    CrossHatch,        ///< Both diagonal directions
    Horizontal,        ///< Horizontal lines
    Vertical           ///< Vertical lines
};
```

Hatching can be implemented via:

1. **Fragment shader approach**: A shader that discards fragments based on position modulo
   hatch spacing and angle. This is efficient but requires a new shader variant.
2. **Additional geometry approach**: Generate hatch lines as `RenderablePolyLineBatch` clipped
   to the interval rectangle. Simpler but generates more geometry.

The fragment shader approach is preferred for performance with many intervals.

### 6.3 SVG Export Update

Update `SVGExporter` to support border and hatching in SVG output. SVG natively supports
`stroke`, `stroke-width`, and `<pattern>` elements for hatching, making this straightforward.

### 6.4 Properties Panel Controls

Add to the interval section of `DataViewerPropertiesWidget`:

- **Fill alpha** slider (0.0–1.0)
- **Border thickness** spinbox
- **Border color** button
- **Hatch pattern** combo box
- **Hatch spacing** spinbox (enabled when pattern ≠ None)

---

## Phase 7: Analog Line Style Enhancements {#phase-7-analog-line-style}

**Status**: Partially implemented. Color, alpha, and line thickness exist in
`AnalogSeriesOptionsData` via `CorePlotting::SeriesStyle`. Gap handling modes exist
but are basic.

**Goal**: Additional line rendering options for publication-quality analog traces.

### 7.1 Planned Options

```cpp
struct AnalogSeriesOptionsData {
    // Existing via SeriesStyle
    // hex_color, alpha, line_thickness, is_visible

    // Existing analog-specific
    // user_scale_factor, y_offset, gap_handling, enable_gap_detection, gap_threshold

    // New options
    LineStyle line_style = LineStyle::Solid;  ///< Solid, Dashed, Dotted
    bool show_data_points = false;            ///< Overlay point markers on the line
    PointGlyphType data_point_glyph = PointGlyphType::Circle;
    float data_point_size = 3.0f;
};

enum class LineStyle {
    Solid,    ///< Continuous line (default)
    Dashed,   ///< Dashed segments
    Dotted    ///< Dotted segments
};
```

### 7.2 Rendering

Dashed/dotted styles can be implemented via fragment shader stipple patterns or by breaking
the polyline into segments during scene building. The shader approach is preferred for
consistency with interval hatching (Phase 6).

---

## Phase 8: Shared Component Extraction {#phase-8-shared-components}

**Status**: Not started. Design exploration — depends on Phases 1–6 establishing which patterns
are truly shared.

**Goal**: Extract DataViewer-specific implementations that have general utility into shared
locations, and adopt shared components that are currently duplicated.

### 8.1 Components to Extract from DataViewer

| Component | Current Location | Target Location | Also Used By |
|-----------|-----------------|-----------------|--------------|
| `SeriesOptionsRegistry` | `DataViewer_Widget/Core/` | `Plots/Common/` or `CorePlotting/` | Any multi-series plot widget |
| `TransformComposers` | `DataViewer_Widget/Rendering/` | `CorePlotting/Layout/` | Any widget with stacked lane layout |
| `AnalogVertexCache` | `DataViewer_Widget/Rendering/` | `CorePlotting/` or `PlottingOpenGL/` | [LinePlotWidget](../LinePlotWidget/index.qmd) (dense time series) |
| `DataViewerCoordinates` | `DataViewer_Widget/Interaction/` | Pattern generalizable via `Plots/Common/` | Layout+coordinate queries shared |
| `SpikeSorterConfigLoader` | `DataViewer_Widget/Core/` | `DataManager/` or `CorePlotting/` | Any widget displaying multi-channel data |

### 8.2 Components to Adopt from Plots/Common/

| Shared Component | Current DataViewer Equivalent | Adoption Notes |
|-----------------|------------------------------|----------------|
| `VerticalAxisState` + `VerticalAxisWidget` | Direct `y_min`/`y_max` in state | Phase 2.1 |
| `CenteredTimeAxis` (new) | Direct time window in state | Phase 4.1 |
| `PlotInteractionHelpers` | Custom zoom/pan in `DataViewerInputHandler` | Adopt shared zoom/pan concepts |

### 8.3 Shared Visual Options Infrastructure

The point glyph options infrastructure is now implemented and centralized:

```
CorePlotting/DataTypes/
├── GlyphStyleData.hpp         # COMPLETE: GlyphType enum + GlyphStyleData struct
├── GlyphStyleConversion.hpp   # COMPLETE: toRenderableGlyphType(), hexColorToVec4()
├── SeriesStyle.hpp            # Already exists: hex_color, alpha, line_thickness, is_visible
├── LineRenderingOptions.hpp   # Planned: line_style (solid/dashed/dotted), data_point overlay
├── IntervalRenderingOptions.hpp # Planned: border, fill_alpha, hatch pattern

Plots/Common/GlyphStyleWidget/
├── Core/GlyphStyleState.hpp   # COMPLETE: QObject wrapper with styleChanged/styleUpdated signals
├── GlyphStyleControls.hpp     # COMPLETE: Widget with shape combo, size spinbox, color button, alpha
├── CMakeLists.txt             # COMPLETE: Static library linking Qt6::Widgets + CorePlotting
```

Already integrated into: TemporalProjectionViewWidget, OnionSkinViewWidget, EventPlotWidget.

Remaining shared visual option structs to implement:

- **DataViewer Widget**: Events (Phase 5), Intervals (Phase 6), Analog lines (Phase 7)
- **[EventPlotWidget](../EventPlotWidget/roadmap.qmd)**: Phase 3 glyph options
- **[OnionSkinViewWidget](../OnionSkinViewWidget/roadmap.qmd)**: Phase 1 point options, Phase 2 line options
- **[ScatterPlotWidget](../ScatterPlotWidget/roadmap.qmd)**: Phase 4 point options
- **[TemporalProjectionViewWidget](../TemporalProjectionViewWidget/roadmap.qmd)**: Phase 1 point options

---

## Phase 9: Directory Relocation {#phase-9-directory-relocation}

**Status**: Not started. Low priority — functional correctness is unaffected.

**Goal**: Move `src/WhiskerToolbox/DataViewer_Widget/` to `src/WhiskerToolbox/Plots/DataViewerWidget/`
for consistency with other plot widgets.

### 9.1 Steps

1. Move directory `DataViewer_Widget/` → `Plots/DataViewerWidget/`
2. Update `CMakeLists.txt` references in `src/WhiskerToolbox/` and the widget's own `CMakeLists.txt`
3. Update `#include` paths across the codebase
4. Update registration module type ID if it references the path
5. Update test directory structure in `tests/` if mirrored

This is a purely mechanical refactor with no behavioral change. It should be done after the
major feature phases to avoid merge conflicts.

---

## Implementation Priority

### Completed Prerequisites

- **[Developer Debug Panel](developer_debug_panel.qmd)** — Diagnostic infrastructure providing
  real-time visibility into lane layout, scales, coordinate transforms, and viewport state.
  **Complete** — accelerates development of Phases 1–3.

### Next Up (Y-Axis Track — Highest User Impact)

1. **Phase 1**: Layout foundation — lane sizing model that all Y-axis work depends on
   ([#248](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/248),
   [#227](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/227))
2. **Phase 1B**: Rendering pipeline modernization — migrate to `ViewStateData`,
   add scene dirty guard, adopt shared interaction helpers for fast pan/zoom
3. **Phase 2**: Y-axis navigation — pan/zoom, culling, wheel modifiers
   ([#227](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/227),
   [#248](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/248))
4. **Phase 3**: Y-axis scalebars and lane labels
   ([#247](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/247))

### Parallel Track (X-Axis Extraction)

4. **Phase 4**: CenteredTimeAxis widget — can proceed in parallel with Phase 3
   ([#223](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/223))

### Following (Visual Enhancements)

5. **Phase 5**: Enhanced event glyph options — shared infrastructure milestone
6. **Phase 6**: Enhanced interval rendering — border, alpha, hatching
7. **Phase 7**: Analog line style enhancements

### Deferred

8. **Phase 8**: Shared component extraction — house-cleaning across widgets
9. **Phase 9**: Directory relocation — mechanical cleanup

---

## Open Questions

1. **CenteredTimeAxis scope**: Should the new axis variant handle the time-following logic
   itself (receiving `timeChanged` signals), or should it remain a pure zoom widget with the
   center set externally? The external approach is more flexible but requires the parent widget
   to manually update the center on every time change.

2. **Lane height policy**: Should lane height be fixed across all series types, or should
   analog channels get taller lanes than event/interval channels by default? A configurable
   policy (uniform vs. type-weighted) could address both preferences.

3. **Viewport culling granularity**: Should culling operate at the series level (skip entire
   series) or could it operate at the sample level (skip analog samples outside the viewport)?
   Sample-level culling is already partially handled by the `AnalogVertexCache` time range
   queries.

4. **Hatching renderer**: Fragment shader stipple vs. additional geometry — the shader approach
   is more performant but adds shader complexity. Is the interval count high enough to
   warrant shader-based hatching, or would geometry-based hatching suffice?

5. **SeriesOptionsRegistry scope**: If extracted to `Plots/Common/`, should it remain specific
   to the three DataViewer series types (analog, event, interval), or should it be generalized
   to support arbitrary option types via a compile-time type list?

6. **Y-axis wheel modifier chords**: Which modifier combinations should be used for Y-axis
   operations? `Alt+Scroll` is a natural candidate but may conflict with Linux window manager
   defaults (Alt+click is often window move). Alternatives include right-click+scroll or
   other combinations.

### Resolved Questions

- **Multi-lane VerticalAxisWidget design** (was Q6): **Decision — new separate widget.**
  `MultiLaneVerticalAxisWidget` will be a standalone widget in `Plots/Common/`, not a subclass
  or extension of the existing `VerticalAxisWidget`. The single-range widget remains unchanged
  for consumers that need a uniform Y range.

- **CenteredTimeAxis state representation** (was Q8): **Decision — `time_start`/`time_end`.**
  Consistent with `TimeSeriesViewState`. The State class provides `halfWindow()` and `center()`
  convenience accessors computed from `time_start`/`time_end`.

---

# See Also

- [Data Viewer Widget Documentation](index.qmd) — Full architecture and feature documentation
- [Developer Debug Panel Plan](developer_debug_panel.qmd) — Diagnostic panel and on-canvas overlays for development
- [Plot Widget Guide](../plot_widget_guide.md) — Shared axis architecture, DataManager integration, testing patterns
- [EventPlotWidget Roadmap](../EventPlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) — Shared glyph infrastructure (Phase 5), cross-widget linking
- [OnionSkinViewWidget Roadmap](../OnionSkinViewWidget/roadmap.qmd) — Shared visual options
- [LinePlotWidget Roadmap](../LinePlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) — Group integration and per-series options patterns
- [ScatterPlotWidget Roadmap](../ScatterPlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) — Shared point glyph infrastructure
- [TemporalProjectionViewWidget Roadmap](../TemporalProjectionViewWidget/roadmap.qmd) — Shared point/line visual options
